samples.xml_description:
Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag indicating the end of multiplication. Shift count register, 0 to 16. Update shift count register. if (!rst_n) begin i <= 0; end else if (start & i < 17) begin i <= i + 1; end else if (!start) begin i <= 0; end Update completion flag. if (!rst_n) begin done_r <= 0; end else if (i == 16) begin done_r <= 1; end else if (i == 17) begin done_r <= 0; end

Initialize registers.

if (!rst_n) begin areg <= 0; breg <= 0; yout_r <= 0; end

Shift and accumulate operation.

if (start) begin if (i == 0) begin areg <= ain; breg <= bin; end else if (i < 17) begin if (areg[i-1]) begin yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1))_0000; end end end

Assign output.

yout <= yout_r;
samples.tb_log:
[COMPILE verilator+cpp]
%Warning-WIDTH: multi_16bit.v:47:46: Operator SHIFTL expects 32 bits on the LHS, but LHS's VARREF 'breg' generates 16 bits.
                                   : ... In instance multi_16bit
   47 |                     yout_r <= yout_r + (breg << (i-1));
      |                                              ^~
                ... Use "/* verilator lint_off WIDTH */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.

[RUN]
